title: Getting Started with Go
content:
### Installing Go

1. Download the latest Go distribution from [go.dev/dl](https://go.dev/dl)
2. Run the installer for your operating system
3. Verify installation:

```bash
go version
```

### Setting Up Your Workspace

In modern Go (1.16+), you don't need a specific `GOPATH` setup. Just create a module:

```bash
mkdir myproject
cd myproject
go mod init myproject
```

### Your First Program

Create a file named `main.go`:

```go
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
    fmt.Println("Hello, World!")
}
```

### Running Your Program

```bash
go run main.go
```

### Compiling

```bash
go build -o myapp main.go
./myapp
```

### Go Tools

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `go run` | Compile and run |
| `go build` | Compile to binary |
| `go fmt` | Format source code |
| `go vet` | Report suspicious constructs |
| `go test` | Run tests |
| `go mod` | Module management |
| `go doc` | Display documentation |

> **Key Point:** Go 1.x guarantees backward compatibility. Code written for Go 1.0 still compiles on modern versions.

quiz.question: What command initializes a new Go module?
Which command formats Go source code?
